questions.question: Laokhowa Burhachapori Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which city?
questions.explanation: After 40 years, the Laokhowa Wildlife Sanctuary saw the return of one-horned rhinos and is attracting more water birds than Assam’s Deepor Beel Ramsar site. The sanctuary is located in Nagaon District, Assam, covering 70.13 sq.km. It was designated a buffer zone of Kaziranga Tiger Reserve in 2007 and is part of the Laokhowa-Burachapori ecosystem. The sanctuary is surrounded by human-dominated areas on all sides except the north.

questions.question: Which Indian city is the venue of 4th South Asian Senior Athletics Championships 2025?
questions.explanation: Ranchi will host the 4th South Asian Senior Athletics Championships at Birsa Munda Athletics Stadium from May 3 to 5, 2025. This is the first time in 17 years that India is hosting the event, last held in 2008. The event was originally planned for October 2023 but was postponed. Athletes from India, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, and the Maldives will participate.

questions.question: Which day is observed as Global Tourism Resilience Day annually?
questions.explanation: Global Tourism Resilience Day is observed every year on February 17 to highlight the tourism sector’s resilience and adaptability. The day stresses the importance of a strong tourism industry that can recover from setbacks and support economic growth, cultural exchange, and sustainability. Tourism remains vulnerable to global challenges like pandemics, economic crises, and climate change. Established by the UN General Assembly, the day promotes strategies for recovery and collaboration. In 2025, the UN is hosting a global discussion on climate action to advance climate-resilient tourism.

questions.question: Exercise Komodo 2025 is organized by which country?
questions.explanation: Indian Navy’s INS Shardul and P8I aircraft are in Bali, Indonesia, for the International Fleet Review (IFR) 2025 and Exercise Komodo. Exercise Komodo, launched in 2014, is a non-combat multilateral naval drill by the Indonesian Navy. It promotes maritime cooperation and regional security among friendly nations. The 2025 theme is “Maritime Partnership for Peace and Stability.” The fifth edition features fleet reviews, naval drills, officer exchanges, bilateral meetings, and a defense exhibition. It involves 39 countries, with 34 foreign and 18 Indonesian Navy warships participating.

questions.question: Gulf of Eilat, which was seen in news, is located in which sea?
questions.explanation: Recent research reveals a significant pause in coral reef growth in the Gulf of Eilat (Gulf of Aqaba). The Gulf is located at the northern tip of the Red Sea, bordered by Egypt, Israel, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia. It is much deeper (1,850 meters) compared to the shallow Gulf of Suez (100 meters). The Gulf of Aqaba is known for its coral reefs, which are among the northernmost in the world.

questions.question: Which ministry has launched the ‘AI for Entrepreneurship’ micro-learning module?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), and Intel India launched the ‘AI for Entrepreneurship’ micro-learning module. It aims to simplify AI concepts and encourage entrepreneurial thinking among young innovators. The programme targets training 1 lakh youth by 2025 to help them succeed in a technology-driven economy. The module focuses on AI for business growth, entrepreneurial strategies, and problem-solving. It is designed for both urban and rural learners to ensure inclusivity.

questions.question: Which institution has organized the ‘Waste Recycling and Climate Change 2025’ conference?

options.label: B
options.text: Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE)

options.label: C
options.text: Recycling and Environment Industries Association of India (REIAI)

answer.label: C
answer.text: Recycling and Environment Industries Association of India (REIAI)

questions.explanation: Union Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change inaugurated the ‘Waste Recycling and Climate Change 2025’ conference. It is organized by Recycling and Environment Industries Association of India (REIAI). India generates 62 million tonnes of waste annually, with plastic, electronic, and hazardous waste rapidly increasing. The program aims to encourage product redesign, invest in recycling technologies, strengthen supply chains, and increase consumer awareness.

questions.question: What is Bacterial Cellulose that was recently mentioned in news?

options.label: A
options.text: A natural polymer produced by certain bacteria

options.label: B
options.text: A fungal byproduct found in soil

answer.label: A
answer.text: A natural polymer produced by certain bacteria

questions.explanation: Researchers found that bacterial cellulose can be used as a bandage to help plants heal and regenerate faster. It is a natural polymer produced by certain bacteria. It has unique properties that make it effective for plant wound care. Bacterial cellulose bandages offer a sustainable alternative to chemical treatments. This method supports healthier plant growth and eco-friendly farming. It enhances plant healing, leading to better crop yields and resilience. This breakthrough integrates biotechnology into agriculture, promoting sustainable farming and food security.

questions.question: Nauru Island, which was seen in news, is located in which ocean?
questions.explanation: Nauru, a small island nation in the Pacific Ocean, plans to sell citizenships to fund the relocation of 10,000 residents threatened by rising sea levels. It is the world’s smallest republic, covering 8 square miles, with a population of 12,500. It is located 1,300 km northeast of the Solomon Islands, its closest neighbor is Banaba in Kiribati. A fossilized coral atoll, it is one of the three great phosphate rock islands in the Pacific.

questions.question: Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS) is associated with which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS) has reopened for applications with Round 2 of the pilot phase. The scheme aims to offer 12-month paid internships in top Indian companies to individuals aged 21-24, not enrolled in full-time academic programs. The Ministry of Corporate Affairs oversees PMIS. Interns receive ₹5,000 monthly, plus a one-time ₹6,000 assistance. Participants gain real-life experience in leading companies. Insurance coverage is provided through Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana and Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana.
